commit_scope: Promote ordinary substrate compiler traffic and runtime event writes through BML-authored Go kernel routes

commands: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/json.fk form-stdlib/cache.fk form-stdlib/form-ontology-loader.fk form-stdlib/source-compiler.fk form-stdlib/kernel-http.fk form-stdlib/language-model.fk form-stdlib/tests/source-language-route-class-template-band.fk
local_validation.summary: POST /api/substrate/form is no longer gated by X-Form-Compiler; ordinary JSON compiler calls route through the Go kernel and BML handler. POST /api/runtime/events now has a BML handler that validates request JSON, normalizes endpoint/raw_endpoint, computes runtime_cost_estimate from config, writes to production Postgres through pg_exec, and emits JSON through Form json-emit. The live native curl returned 201 and public API readback found the inserted production row.

known_failed_attempts.command: curl http://127.0.0.1:19091/api/runtime/events before opening the SSH tunnel
known_failed_attempts.output: The BML route matched natively but returned 503 database unavailable because ~/.coherence-network/secrets/form-kernel-postgres-tunnel.json points to 127.0.0.1:15432 and the tunnel was closed. Opened the documented Hostinger SSH tunnel and retried successfully.

known_failed_attempts.command: curl http://127.0.0.1:19091/api/runtime/events with native_observation.cost_model response field
known_failed_attempts.output: A nonessential diagnostic string in a deeply nested BML response emitted malformed text or miscompiled when moved behind a helper. Removed the diagnostic field; the cost calculation still reads agent_cost.runtime_cost_per_second from config.

ci_validation.reason: No commit, push, or PR was created in this turn.

deploy_validation.reason: No production deploy was performed. Production database proof used the local Go front door over the documented SSH tunnel.

phase_gate.reason: Local proof and production DB write proof passed, but CI and deploy validation remain pending until this work is committed and shipped.

evidence_refs: native_route_goal_loop /loop --source api reports bml_routes=29, high-grammar native share=0.8390, native-executable share=0.8530, next gap GET /api/gates/main-head.
POST /api/runtime/events disappeared as a POST promotion gap; the remaining /api/runtime/events row is GET/list traffic.
Go route test proves ordinary POST /api/substrate/form returns 200, X-Form-Router=native-kernel-go, handler=api_substrate_form, and value=42.
Go route test proves POST /api/runtime/events validation returns 422 through X-Form-Router=native-kernel-go without fanout.
Live native curl through Go front door returned HTTP 201 for POST /api/runtime/events with id rt_d572fb0bbee0, endpoint /api/runtime/events, method POST, origin_idea_id oss-interface-alignment.
Public API readback found rt_d572fb0bbee0 in /api/runtime/events?source=api&limit=20&force_refresh=true.
Header proof on validation request returned X-Form-Route-Where: route:runtime-events-create pattern:/api/runtime/events request:/api/runtime/events.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: High-traffic compiler POSTs and runtime event creation can enter through the Go native front door with handlers written in BML. Runtime event creation performs a real production Postgres write without adding JSON encoding or route-specific business logic to the kernel carrier.
test_flows: Ordinary substrate compiler POST compiles and runs a BML expression through the Go kernel and returns value 42.
Runtime event POST validates malformed source through the native route without fanout.
Runtime event POST writes a real production row through the Go front door and the public API reads the same row back.
